description: Audio Stream Metadata is a component that reads the running tallies of the different samples being sent and received by an AudioStream`1.

fields.Stream: The audio source to get data from
fields.UnreadSamples: The amount of samples yet to be played
fields.TotalMissedSamples: Samples missed due to lag or other reasons.
fields.LastMissedSamples: The last time samples were missed instead of being read from the audio source.
fields.BufferLength: How big the sample buffer is.
fields.AverageReadSamplesPerSecond: How many samples per second on a running average are being processed.
fields.AverageWriteSamplesPerSecond: The many samples per second on a running average are being written to the buffer per second.
fields.GlobalIndex: The audio source's index in the pool of audio players playing sounds.
fields.SamplesAvailableForEncode: Samples currently ready to be encoded to a format supported by FrooxEngine's audio system.
fields.FrameSize: The size of audio data coming in, which is in bytes and is influenced by data and channel count.
fields.MaxFrameSize: The max size of audio data coming in, which is in bytes and is influenced by data and channel count.
fields.EncodedSampleRate: how fast encoded samples are being processed.
fields.TotalPacketCount: How many audio packets have been networked by the audio source so far.
fields.TotalLostPackets: How many packets of the network that were not received from the audio source.
fields.LastLostPackets: The last time packets were lost by the audio source.
fields.PacketLossRatio: On average, the percentage of audio packets from the network that are being lost.
fields.AverageCodecSamplesPerSecond: The average amount of samples incoming in encoded form.
